risks: Triple-scoop on the core contribution: V-STAR (2604.10219) already delivers the temporal-commitment finding + targeted intervention + zero overhead on the SAME model (Qwen2.5-VL-7B); PSRD and Focus Matters cover the LLaVA-1.5-7B case. The 'commitment analysis is the scientific finding' pillar is not novel as of mid-2026.

Mechanistic premise is likely mis-specified: prior work shows hallucination recurs at EACH phase/pivot (recurring high-entropy nodes), not a single attractor 'lock-in.' A one-shot deferral at 'the' commitment step is probably strictly weaker than V-STAR/PSRD's per-node reactivation.

Premise-vs-evidence conflict: HIVE (the author's own motivation) shows hallucinated semantics frequently AID downstream VLM accuracy, so deferral at the commitment step risks lowering OOD/VQA accuracy — the exact side effect the proposal claims to fix.

Clean-win probability against an honest 2027 baseline set is low: differentiation reduces to 'deferral/abstain instead of re-grounding' — a thin sliver that reads as an incremental variant of V-STAR, and abstention typically trades accuracy for calibration (weaker headline numbers).

Even a positive result would likely be framed by reviewers as 'multimodal port of 2604.15400 + a decoding intervention already dominated by V-STAR/PSRD' — an incremental-combination rejection risk regardless of execution quality.

refute.strongest_kill_shot: Total novelty pre-emption by V-STAR (arxiv:2604.10219, "Cognitive Pivot Points and Visual Anchoring," updated 2026-05-28), which already delivers the proposal's exact headline triad on the proposal's own base model. V-STAR (a) identifies discrete temporal "cognitive bifurcation / pivot points" in a MULTIMODAL reasoning chain where >70% of hallucinations emerge (entropy spikes at transition tokens), (b) frames this as attractor-like "cognitive inertia" with intermediate-layer visual-attention collapse preceding lock-in, and (c) intervenes ONLY at those points via targeted re-grounding (HVAR) + forced reflection (FRM), reporting SOTA on Qwen2.5-VL-7B across 40+ benchmarks at near-zero overhead. This is C3's "commitment step + attractor + targeted-only intervention beats uniform at a fraction of overhead," on C3's exact model. C3's central novelty premise — that commitment/attractor dynamics are TEXT-only (2604.15400) and untreated for multimodal CoT — is therefore factually false as of mid-2026, and the prior-art list that omits V-STAR and 2603.27201 ("divergent thinking" step-localized decoding intervention) will not survive Reviewer 1's first literature pass. A head-to-head against V-STAR/PSRD is unavoidable and C3 is positioned as a weaker, cheaper variant.
refute.notes:
Kill-shot is fatal to novelty, not to feasibility — hence survives=false despite a technically buildable project. All four lenses independently converge on the same scoop (2604.10219 V-STAR, 2603.27201 divergent-thinking, 2604.17982 PSRD, 2604.03556 Focus Matters), so this is not a single-reviewer risk but a consensus reject on Rule-1 grounds.

Residual novelty (~0.1): after the scoop, the only differentiators are (a) deferral/abstention instead of re-grounding — a thin action-swap over V-STAR's re-anchoring and the existing abstention line (2604.14799, 2606.16667), and (b) a measured side-effect profile (refusal-up/OOD-down/overhead) — which is an evaluation/measurement contribution explicitly disqualified as a primary contribution. Leaning harder on "commitment analysis as THE scientific finding" worsens the gap because that is the most thoroughly scooped part.

Two compounding weaknesses beyond the scoop: (1) Mechanism likely mis-specified — PSRD and Focus Matters show hallucination recurs at EACH phase/pivot onset, not a single attractor "lock-in," so a one-shot deferral at "the" commitment step is plausibly strictly weaker than per-node reactivation. (2) Premise-vs-evidence conflict — C3's own motivating citation HIVE (2607.07507) finds hallucinated semantics frequently AID downstream VLM accuracy, so deferral at the commitment step risks LOWERING OOD/VQA accuracy, a cheap <10-GPU-h falsification the authors have not addressed. Realistic outcome vs an honest 2027 baseline set is a TIE, not a clean win.

Residual feasibility (~0.78): high and largely independent of the kill-shot. 7B models fit on one L40; study is inference/probing-heavy with no RL rollouts (the flagged PCIe risk does not apply); ~150-250 GPU-h is well under 600. The real constraint is dev-time: bespoke step-wise activation patching with live KV-cache surgery across two VLM architectures (2-3 wks), Qwen2.5-VL dynamic-resolution/variable-token count making a standardized cross-prompt commitment-step index hard to define, and an unbudgeted hallucination-onset annotation pipeline. Feasible to build, but feasibility cannot rescue a pre-empted contribution.

ranked.one_line: REPAIRED (substrate pivot): abandon dead adaptive-latent-depth; instead give a mechanistic, training-free, per-instance-gated fix for the verified self-reflection 'illusion' (2605.15864) — the visual-attention surge is gated by token role/provenance, and restoring the user-role profile on a few heads recovers the self-vs-user accuracy gap without global-steering collateral damage.

Commitment-point hallucination deferral — fully pre-empted by V-STAR (2604.10219) on the same Qwen2.5-VL model (temporal pivot + attractor framing + targeted-only intervention), plus mechanism mis-spec (recurs per-pivot, not single lock-in) and a HIVE premise conflict; consensus reject, kill-shot not reparable.

Perception-vs-reasoning RLVR accounting — both legs (blindfolded-reasoner diagnostic + perception-preserving RL constraint) scooped by MoCA (ICML 2026 Oral, 2605.14054) and again by CapPO (2509.21854); the sole differentiator, 'identifiable' decomposition, is causally false; interlocking scoop, not reparable.

ranking.winner_rationale: All three refute passes returned survives=false, so the decision rule reduces to: which kill-shot is clearly reparable? C2 and C3 are killed by strong single papers that executed the exact contribution on the exact setting (MoCA ICML-Oral for C2; V-STAR for C3) — verified real via search, non-reparable, and their residual novelty is presentational only. C1's kill is different in kind: a PREMISE kill (opaque visual latents collapse/get bypassed, so adaptive latent-DEPTH has nothing to allocate), and premise kills are reparable by changing the substrate rather than the ambition. The repair keeps C1's DNA — per-instance, evidence-sufficiency-gated allocation of visual computation with a formal signal and causal confound control — but swaps the dead substrate (latent CoT depth, verified saturated: Monet/DMLR/ILVR/SCOLAR/2605.18445) for one whose per-instance headroom is VERIFIED real and large: explicit visual re-engagement. 2605.15864 shows a ~50pp self-vs-user re-look gap that the field has only diagnosed. My provenance-search returned no prior work explaining the asymmetry mechanistically or fixing it training-free/per-instance, and the nearest neighbors are either global-and-always-on (CAST/DMAS) or RL-based (V-STAR, 2607.02490) — the latter directly exposed to the unproven PCIe-4xL40 rollout risk that this repair sidesteps entirely. C1-repaired is thus the only candidate that simultaneously (1) neutralizes its own kill-shot with a substrate whose signal provably exists, (2) states a falsifiable mechanism (Rule-1 compliant, not a benchmark), (3) is inference/probing + activation-steering only (no RL) so it fits 4xL40/600h with a week-1 GO/NO-GO, and (4) can win on ACCURACY via a collateral-free comparison against global steering, escaping the efficiency-paper trap. Honest caveat: the neighborhood is crowded, so this is the highest-probability bet among three compromised options, not a slam dunk — hence a week-1 kill-gate is mandatory.
ranking.sharpened_problem_statement: Recent 2026 evidence establishes that VLM self-reflection is largely an illusion: models emit "let me look at the image again," but their attention to visual tokens does not surge and their answers stay anchored to text — VisualSwap shows accuracy dropping up to 60% and thinking models ~3x more vulnerable (2605.15864). Yet the identical instruction delivered as a USER turn does surge visual attention and restores accuracy to ~85%, and the field frames this only as "a failure of control, not capability," offering a user-prompt workaround but no explanation of WHY the same content behaves differently by source and no internal fix. Existing training-free interventions (CAST 2605.04641, DMAS 2602.21704) boost visual attention globally and indiscriminately (risking HIVE-style collateral loss where language priors are correct), while the grounded-re-look methods that work (V-STAR 2604.10219, 2607.02490) require RL. We claim the re-examination surge is gated by token PROVENANCE/role — a small set of role-conditioned attention heads down-weight visual keys during assistant-role (self-generated) decoding, which also explains why more "thinking" makes it worse — and that restoring the user-role attention profile on exactly those heads, only on instances a cheap gate flags as illusion-bound and swap-sensitive, recovers most of the self-vs-user accuracy gap without the collateral loss global steering incurs. This converts C1's falsified "adaptive latent depth on hard images" into a causally-validated, training-free control mechanism on a substrate whose per-instance headroom is independently verified and large.
ranking.core_hypothesis: In VLMs, the visual-attention surge that produces genuine re-examination is gated by the trigger tokens' role/provenance (user-provided vs self-generated), is localized to a small identifiable set of attention heads, and can be restored training-free at self-reflection steps to recover externally-prompted accuracy on swap-sensitive instances without degrading swap-invariant ones.
ranking.method_seed: INPUTS: an open VLM (Qwen2.5-VL-7B primary; Qwen3-VL thinking/instruct as the strongest illusion testbed; LLaVA-1.5-7B with fixed 576 visual tokens for clean head attribution; a second family — InternVL2.5-8B or LLaVA-OneVision-7B — for generality), image+question, and the decoded reasoning trace. MECHANISM STUDY (no learning, week 1): with the re-look instruction's CONTENT held fixed, measure per-head visual-attention mass under three matched conditions — (a) self-generated mid-decoding, (b) injected as a user turn, (c) injected as assistant-role at a turn boundary — to dissociate ROLE/provenance from position/turn-boundary; identify the head set whose visual-attention responds to role, yielding the falsifiable "role-conditioned visual-routing heads" claim. LEARNED COMPONENT: a lightweight per-instance gate g(x) — a small supervised probe (no RL, tiny external classifier) on cheap pre-trigger features (current visual-attention profile on the identified heads vs the empirically-measured user-triggered reference profile, plus answer-logit margin) that predicts whether the instance is in the illusory-inertia regime AND swap-sensitive. TRAINING SIGNAL: self-supervised oracle labels from a training split — run user-role re-look vs no re-look and label "benefits" where external re-look flips the answer to correct or where VisualSwap detection succeeds only with re-engagement; g(x) learns to predict this from internal features; threshold calibrated to a target intervention budget. INFERENCE-TIME BEHAVIOR: during decoding, at re-look/self-reflection steps, evaluate g(x); if it fires, apply a training-free provenance-restoring intervention on exactly the identified heads (rescale their visual-key attention to the measured user-role profile, or role/positional-spoof the trigger span) for the continuation; otherwise decode normally — zero added parameters, no user in the loop, targeted (not global), gated (not always-on).
ranking.primary_claim: A training-free, per-instance-gated provenance-restoring intervention on a small set of role-conditioned attention heads recovers the majority of the self-vs-user visual-re-examination accuracy gap (documented by 2605.15864) on swap-sensitive instances — approaching the user-prompt oracle upper bound — WITHOUT the collateral accuracy loss on swap-invariant instances that global visual-attention steering (CAST 2605.04641, DMAS 2602.21704) incurs, and without RL; this establishes the illusion as a role-gated control failure rather than a capability limit.
recommended_models: Qwen3-VL instruct + thinking variants (strongest illusion signal; thinking ~3x more vulnerable per 2605.15864)
LLaVA-1.5-7B (fixed 576 visual tokens -> clean, stable per-head attribution; addresses the dynamic-token confound flagged for Qwen)
InternVL2.5-8B or LLaVA-OneVision-7B (second architecture family for a cross-model generality claim)
Qwen2.5-VL-3B (optional; full-FT-feasible variant, though the gate is a tiny external probe so full-FT is not required)
recommended_datasets: VS-Bench / VisualSwap image-swap protocol (2605.15864) — the causal control for genuine vs illusory re-examination
POPE (object-existence hallucination) split into swap-sensitive vs swap-invariant slices
MMVP (fine-grained perception where vision is decisive)
GQA / A-OKVQA / TextVQA (general VQA; slice by swap-sensitivity to measure both recovery and collateral)
top_risks: Crowded neighborhood: reviewers will demand head-to-heads with 2605.15864 (diagnosis), CAST/DMAS/VLI/Spectral-QK (training-free visual-attention steering), V-STAR and 2607.02490 (RL grounded re-look), and Look-Back 2507.03019 (implicit re-focusing). Novelty rests ENTIRELY on the provenance MECHANISM + gated targeting being demonstrably superior to generic steering; a weak mechanism story collapses this into 'yet another attention-steering hallucination paper.'

Confound: the surge may track POSITION/recency/turn-boundary rather than user-vs-assistant ROLE, narrowing or invalidating the 'provenance' framing (still salvageable as turn-boundary gating, but weaker and less novel).

Partial recovery: restoring the user-role attention profile internally may not reproduce the external-prompt upper bound (the user turn's KV context may matter beyond role), downgrading the headline from 'recovers most of the gap' to a partial result.

HIVE-style collateral: intervening where language priors were already correct can LOWER accuracy on swap-invariant instances; the gate must be precise enough that the net joint win over global steering survives — otherwise it ties.

Head-localization generality: the effect may be clean on LLaVA-1.5 (fixed tokens) but diffuse/messy on Qwen dynamic-resolution/variable-token models, jeopardizing the cross-architecture 'general mechanism' claim (the same fragility flagged against C3).
kill_conditions: ROLE-vs-POSITION test (week 1, <10 GPU-h, GO/NO-GO): in the 3-condition matched-content design, if the visual-attention surge tracks turn-boundary/position rather than user-vs-assistant role, the provenance mechanism is mis-attributed — detect immediately and either reframe to positional/turn-boundary gating or stop.

Head-localization check (week 1-2): if no small head set carries the role effect (it is model-wide/diffuse), the targeted training-free fix is likely indistinguishable from global steering (CAST/DMAS) and loses its differentiator — detect via per-head causal attribution before building the gate.

VisualSwap internal-trigger validation (week 1-2, inference-only): if the internal provenance-restoring intervention fails to raise VisualSwap detection / accuracy toward the user-prompt oracle, the core claim is dead — this is the single cheapest decisive experiment; run it first.

Collateral-free-win head-to-head (by week 4): if the gated intervention cannot beat global steering on the JOINT metric (recover swap-sensitive AND preserve swap-invariant), the clean-win claim degrades to a tie/efficiency result — abort or reframe.

Scoop re-check (week 1 close-read): if 2607.02490 ('Visually Grounded Self-Reflection via RL'), Look-Back (2507.03019), or a newer preprint already delivers a training-free role/provenance mechanism + per-instance-gated fix validated by swap-detection, novelty collapses — read these end-to-end before committing.
